message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: My feature: add process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class in these two files. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing click.edit() multi-file support (edit_file -> edit_files). Need these two files.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
your_files: src/click/termui.py
note: Since both our features touch both files, let's split BY FILE instead: I own _termui_impl.py (Editor class - I'll add process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and apply it when spawning the subprocess). You own termui.py (edit() function - please add your edit_files support there, AND also add a process_priority=None passthrough param to click.edit() that forwards to Editor(..., process_priority=process_priority)). This keeps file edits disjoint. OK with this split?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Both features touch the same edit()/Editor code, true file-disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: I'll add edit_files()/multi-filename support (rename edit_file->edit_files, filename param becomes str|Iterable[str]|None, subprocess call builds multiple quoted args). I will NOT touch Editor.__init__ signature or add any priority/nice-related code. Please add process_priority only to Editor.__init__, the edit()/edit_files() body (just passing priority through to subprocess, e.g. via os.nice/psutil or similar), and the termui.edit() call to Editor(...) - but please don't rename edit_file or change the filename param type, since that's mine. If you go first or I go first, whoever implements second should just re-add their small piece on top. Sound OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Accepted. Concretely: I will only touch (1) Editor.__init__ signature to add process_priority=None param + store it, (2) inside edit()/edit_files() subprocess-launch code, add priority application (os.nice on Unix / psutil or ctypes SetPriorityClass on Windows) wrapped in try/except so failures are silent, without renaming edit_file or changing the filename param type/signature otherwise, (3) in termui.py, add process_priority=None param to click.edit() and pass it through to Editor(...). I will not touch edit_files multi-filename logic. Will implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
your_files: src/click/termui.py
note: Swap vs your last message: I own _termui_impl.py (Editor class), you own termui.py. Reason: my core work (rename edit_file->edit_files, build multi-arg subprocess command) IS the Editor class body in _termui_impl.py, so I can't implement it if you own that file. Since you own termui.py: please make these two small edits there for my feature (in addition to your process_priority param): (1) change edit()'s filename param type to 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None', (2) change the call at the bottom from 'ed.edit_file(filename); return None' to 'ed.edit_files(filename); return None' (I'll keep edit_files accepting a single str too for back-compat). I will not touch termui.py at all. Deal?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Accepted, functional split confirmed. Clarification: I'll make the filename type change and edit_files() call myself in termui.py (since you didn't confirm doing it) - I'll only touch the 'filename' param annotation and the final ed.edit_file(...)->ed.edit_files(...) call, leaving your process_priority param/passthrough and Editor.__init__ additions untouched. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished my part: added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ (_termui_impl.py) with _NICE_VALUES/_WIN_PRIORITY_CLASSES maps, applied in edit_file() via os.setpriority on Unix and Popen creationflags on Windows, all wrapped to fail silently. Added process_priority=None param to click.edit() in termui.py, passed through to Editor(...). Did NOT touch edit_file's rename or filename param type - that's yours. Tests pass (62 passed in test_termui.py).

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished my part: renamed Editor.edit_file->edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]), builds a shell command with multiple quoted filenames via subprocess. Editor.edit() now calls edit_files((name,)). In termui.py: filename param now accepts str | Iterable[str] | None, click.edit() wraps a single str into a tuple before calling ed.edit_files(). Did not touch process_priority/Editor.__init__. Tests in test_termui.py pass (62 passed). Verified single- and multi-file cases manually with mocked subprocess.Popen.
